description:
2025 | 15 | 1h 59m
A writer tries to reconnect with his sons. Based on the novel by David Gilbert. Starring Dominic West, Bill Nighy and George MacKay
name: & Sons

description:
2025 | 15 | 1h 47m
The renowned psychiatrist Lilian Steiner mounts a private investigation into the death of one of her patients, whom she is convinced has been murdered.
name: A Private Life

description:
As long as this heart lives, so too will Kilburn.

Kilburn is at a crossroads. Local institutions are being closed, The Suits are sniffing around, and there's talk of 'rejuvenation'. When out of nowhere, a mysterious object presents itself - the bloody, beating heart of Kilburn.
 
As excitement around this unprecedented discovery spreads, the community is under threat, can the people of Kilburn come together and hold on to what's theirs?
 
In this flagship community production, Kiln's Intergenerational Company, made up of 80 cast members ranging from ages 6-88, take to the mainstage to kick off the new season..
name: ALL THE THINGS WE CAN'T EXPLAIN

description: Supplementary event for audio described performances
name: Audio Description Add-ons
attribute_EventDetails: Please book only if you require Hearing Enhancement or Audio Description

description: 1998 - 1hr 22 mins - 15 - BABYMOTHER is a musical drama, from 1998, in which a young mother struggles in her attempts to find recognition and success as a singer.
name: Babymother

description:
One night, two lives and a past that refuses to leave.
 
Tom is travelling in Berlin with no hostel and no plan. When Charlotte, a native Berliner working in a bar, offers him the use of her couch, he can't believe his luck.
 
Intoxicated by one another, they want to lose themselves to desire. But as night becomes morning, secrets emerge that cannot be ignored.
 
Joanna Murray-Smith’s gripping, contemporary thriller makes it UK debut following an acclaimed Australian premiere and asks is it better to remember or to forget?
name: BERLIN

description:
2025 | 12A | 1h 31m
A family of six moves into a new home on Vancouver Island, but the eldest son's disruptive behaviour gradually throws their lives off balance.
name: Blue Heron

description: 2020 - U - 1hr 46mins - Recording of a live performance which incorporates dance and music from the Broadway musical.
name: David Byrne's American Utopia

description: 2hrs 30mins (including 20 mins interval) - Written by Martina Laird - Diamond is on the run from his life in a post war Trinidad. In the pursuit of the mother who abandoned him, he finds himself trapped between desire and political entanglement. The turns in his small-time con act echo the developments in the nation’s colonial legacy. His own challenges for self-determination are set against the British colony’s struggle to free itself from Empire and move towards Independence.
name: DRIFTWOOD

description: 2025 - 1hr 39 mins - 12A - Documentary about a 2021 protest on a street in Glasgow where local people staged a standoff with Home Office immigration officials to prevent the deportation of two Sikh men.
name: Everybody to Kenmure Street

description:
2024 - 12a - 1h 30m
An octogenarian woman transitions to life in assisted living as she contends with her conflicting relationship to herself and her caregivers amidst her shifting memory, age identity, and desires.
name: Familiar Touch

description:
1hr 50mins, including 20 mins interval

Doors: 6:30pm
Performance Start: 7:00pm
Performance End: 8:50pm
Post show book signing
Curfew: 10:30pm
name: Fane X FairyLoot Presents: Adalyn Grace and The Wretched Divine

description:
15 | 2h16m 

GIANT, the 5-star critically acclaimed play starring John Lithgow (Killers of the Flower Moon, The
Crown) as Roald Dahl, comes to the big screen. Winner of 3 Olivier Awards® including Best Play and a Tony Award® for Best Actor in a Play (John Lithgow), the production was filmed live in London’s
West End ahead of its sold-out Broadway run. The stellar cast includes Aya Cash (The Boys, The
Franchise, You’re the Worst), Elliot Levey (Cold War, Patriots, Good) and Rachael Stirling (The Divine
Mrs S).
A world-famous children’s author under threat. A battle of wills in the wake of scandal. And one
chance to make amends...It’s the summer of 1983, The Witches is about to hit the shelves and Roald
Dahl is making last-minute edits. But the outcry at his recent, explicitly antisemitic article won’t die
down. Across a single afternoon at his family home, and rocked by an unexpectedly explosive
confrontation, Dahl is forced to choose: make a public apology or risk his name and reputation.
Inspired by real events, GIANT explores with dark humour the difference between considered
opinion and dangerous rhetoric offering a complicated portrait of a fiendishly charismatic icon.
name: GIANT – The Play

description: A guided ticket for accessible bookers to Passages 1
name: Guided Ticket (Accessible)

description: 2025 - 1hr 29mins - Two best friends face the end of childhood when racial profiling and a traumatic police stop-and-search tear them apart, teaching Ish about letting go in a society where brown boys are constantly watched.
name: Ish

description:
1996 - 15 - 1hr 48mins

Jim Carter returns to Kiln Theatre for a special fundraiser and screening of the modern British classic Brassed Off, celebrating the 30th anniversary of this heartfelt story of solidarity and resilience.  

Following the screening, there will be a Q&A with producer Steve Abbott and cast members Tara Fitzgerald, Philip Jackson and Stephen Tompkinson, hosted by fellow cast member Jim Carter.

Yorkshire, 1992. As the last of the pits faces closure, the future of the Grimley Colliery Brass Band is in peril, alongside the identity of the town and the livelihood of its people. When a new flugelhorn player joins the band, leader Danny decides to enter a national music competition as their last chance for glory – which may just be what it takes to save community spirit.  

All funds raised will go towards sustaining our work with local communities and supporting our productions
name: Jim Carter presents: Brassed Off 30th Anniversary Screening + Q&A

description:
25 mins -
Location: Kilburn High Road
Dates: 4-8 August
Timings: 1pm, 1.45pm, 2.30pm, 3.30pm, 6.15pm & 7pm
Suitable for ages 14+

The second performance is drawn from two submissions about a mutual friend, Keith who lived in Kilburn for all 103 years of his life. Staged within a High Road café, audiences will sit alongside actors and experience Keith’s story through the words of those who knew him. 

Written by Martha Haidari and directed by Imy Wyatt Corner.
name: KEITH

description:
1973 - 59mins - AGE TBC
An in-depth look at Trinidad and Tobago’s carnival through its turbulent history. African and East Indian rhythms and rituals, combined with the European Mardi Gras celebration, have evolved into the spectacle of “mas” and music that erupts on the streets of Port of Spain on the two days preceding Lent.
With CARNIVAL FANTASTIQUE (1960) short Film. 
Q+A with Nicolle-Rachelle Moore in conversation with Jonathan Ali.
name: King Carnival

description: 2007 - 1h 28m - Set locally and based upon Jimmy Murphy’s stage play. Four friends gather for a wake of a friend with whom they made the journey to Camden from Connemara in the 1970s, seeking a better life.
name: Kings

description:
2025 | 15 | 1h 37m
British satirical mockumentary film. 
Lady Isabella, aspires to be in the spotlight, and local talent show 'Stately Stars' provides her a break. However, high pressures and a loss of self lead to a surreal situation on her grand estate.
name: Lady

description: 2017 - U - 1hr 16mins - Lila, a character in a children's story book, suddenly falls out of her paper world and ends up trapped in a place where she doesn't belong... And so starts this great adventure, where Lila discovers that the only person who can save her is Ramon, the owner of the book who used to read her many years ago...   But it's not going to be easy – Ramon is no longer the little boy he once was. He has grown up and has not only stopped reading, but he's also stopped believing in fantasy. Lila and her new friend Manuela set out to convince him of what's happening and together they face a dangerous journey to Oblivion, to retrieve Lila's lost book.    In this adventure through magical worlds, children will explore and discover the true value of friendship and the power of fantasy.
name: Lila's Book

description: 2000 - 2hr 6mins - 12 - LOVE & BASKETBALL is a US romantic drama in which two aspiring basketball players grow up together and fall in love, but are pulled into different directions by their desire to be great.
name: Love and Basketball

description: 1997 - 1hr 49 mins - 15 - LOVE JONES is romance film, from 1997, in which an aspiring poet and a photographer negotiate the early days of their relationship.
name: Love Jones

description:
40 mins
Location: Kilburn High Road
Dates: 11-15 August
Timings: 1.30pm, 3pm, 5.30pm, & 7pm 
Suitable for ages 14+

The third adaptation will take the form of a promenade performance along the High Road. Short scenes and vignettes[EK9.1] will unfold in public spaces, evoking memorable nights out in Kilburn, past and present.  

Written by Nicole Latchana and directed by Ben Grant.
name: Meet Me Here

description:
2026 ‧ Comedy/Adventure ‧ 1h 29m ‧ Family Cinema
Follows the Minions in 1920s Hollywood as they search for frightening creatures for their monster movie, partner with a green creature, and must save the planet after unleashing monsters.
name: Minions and Monsters

description: 2025 - PG - 1 hr 55mins - Live action remake of the 2016 animated adventure in which a Polynesian girl joins forces with a shape-shifting demigod to save her dying island from a curse.
name: Moana

description:
Some secrets don't make it to the grave.

When Gloria passes, her family gathers for the traditional Jamaican Nine Night. And that means the whole family.
As more and more mourners gather bringing rum and curry and complaints, tensions build, and the family must face some uncomfortable truths.

'A spirit never wan fi leave de family home'; and there's still 8 days left…

Amit Sharma (Retrograde, Please Please Me) directs this brand new production of Natasha Gordon's critically acclaimed debut play celebrating the humour and hardships of grief.
name: NINE NIGHT

description:
1973 1h29m 18 
A road movie through 70s Jamaica, which introduces actress Grace Jones. Making their way through the countryside Susan and Carl find themselves attracted to each other. Neither of them, however, has any illusions about the separate and unequal worlds to which they belong.
name: No Place Like Home

description:
3 hours - Among the glittering salons of the super-rich, patriarchy equals power, reputation is everything – and for women, one misstep can mean ruin.

Marquise de Merteuil, master in the art of survival, wields her influence with intelligence and control. Alongside the magnetic Vicomte de Valmont, they turn seduction into strategy and weaponise desire. But when their alliance collapses into rivalry, the battle between them threatens to destroy everyone in their path.
name: NT Live: Les Liaisons Dangereuses

description:
Written by Tom Wright. Running time 1 hour 50mins with 20 min interval interval.

Brian Epstein and The Beatles. Their rise. His fall. 

When Brian Epstein steps into the Cavern Club, he finds more than just a band – he finds his life’s calling. Captivated by the charismatic frontman, John Lennon, Brian sets out to make the Fab Four the most famous group on earth. But beyond the spotlight, he wrestles with secrets, longing, and love.

Please Please Me is the electrifying story of the hidden struggles of Brian Epstein – the unsung, gay legend and ‘Fifth Beatle’.
name: PLEASE PLEASE ME

description:
After a successful career as a political advisor in Westminster, Chloe Dalton’s life had very little to do with the natural world—until the lockdown brought her back to the countryside of her childhood. There, she discovered a seemingly abandoned baby hare and made the life-changing decision to bring it home and care for it.

Her bestselling and critically acclaimed book, Raising Hare, is the story of that transformative experience, and of the extraordinary relationship that developed between human and hare. 

Join Chloe live at the Kiln Theatre, where she will share how caring for animals can rekindle our love for wildlife, and teach us the value of slowing down. She will also answer your questions in the audience Q&A.
name: Raising Hare: An Evening with Chloe Dalton

description:
1978 - 15 - 1h 40m - No trailers, intro at 20:10

A reggae musician in Kingston launches a record-selling venture, but his livelihood is threatened when local criminals steal his motorcycle. He rallies his community to fight back against the gang's intimidation and bring peace.

SHORT FILM: DADLI (2018)
Q+A with Speaker Colin Brown in Conversation with Jonathan Ali
name: Rockers

description: 2022 - 15 - 1h 22m - Two youngsters reeling from bad breakups who connect over an eventful day in South-London.
name: Rye Lane

description:
Two exes. One dinner. What could go wrong? 
 
Would you go to dinner with your ex? The one you were engaged to, but haven't spoken to in years? You're over them - right?! 
 
Jada and Dallas are hashing things out in this sharp, hilarious new comedy. Can they leave 7 years of baggage at the door to give their love a second chance? Is the spark even still there? Or will the weight of their past pull them apart before the night ends?

Following sold out runs in New York and LA, Douglas Lyons' (Chicken & Biscuits) award-winning play, directed by Zhailon Levingston (CATS The Jellicle Ball), makes its UK premiere at Kiln. Featuring Marisha Wallace as Jada and Michael Rishawn as River/Eric, with further casting to be announced, get ready to give love a second chance this autumn.
name: TABLE 17

description: 2026 - 15 - 1hr 47mins Joe and Angela's marriage is on thin ice. When they invite their enigmatic upstairs neighbors for a dinner party, the night spirals into unexpected places.
name: The Invite

description:
2025 - 15 - 1h 56m
A bank robber released from jail must unlock his traumatised brother's memory to recover stolen loot.
name: The Last Viking

description: 2025 - U - 1hr 50 mins - When a family moves to the countryside, the children are reluctant to embrace their new lives until they discover an enchanted wood full of eccentric characters in this fantasy adventure film.
name: The Magic Faraway Tree

description: 2026 - 15 - 2hrs 52mins - After the Trojan War, Odysseus faces a dangerous voyage back to Ithaca, meeting creatures like the Cyclops Polyphemus, Sirens, and Calypso along the way.
name: The Odyssey

description:
1986 | 12 | 2h 29m
At the dawn of World War III, a man searches for a way to restore peace to the world and finds he must give something in return. Director Andrei Tarkovsky
name: The Sacrafice

description: 2025 - U - 1hr 49mins - When a dead body is discovered, a group of sheep suspect foul play and embark upon an investigation to crack the case in this family comedy mystery.
name: The Sheep Detectives

description: 2024 - 1hr 35mins - PG - In this tender drama, a young girl spends the summer on a small Finnish island with her grandmother and father. The film features occasional moments of threat and sensitively explores themes of grief and death.
name: The Summer Book

description:
25 mins - Location: Kilburn High Road
Dates: 31 July – 15 August
Timings: Every hour, 1pm – 7pm
Suitable for ages 14+

The first adaptation celebrates Kilburn’s rich and diverse food culture. Drawing on recordings from restauranteurs, market traders and food businesses, audiences will embark on a guided headphone journey along the High Road. 

The production will be developed in collaboration with Sam Brewer, an Audio Description Consultant, ensuring integrated audio description within the script and making the experience fully accessible for blind and visually impaired audiences. 

Written by Marika McKennell, directed by Sam Potter and Sound Design by Alessandro Quao.
name: This Road

description: 2026 - 1 hr 36 mins - PG - Documentary in which a writer reflects on his connection to glaciers and his late grandparents.
name: Time and Water

description:
2026 | age tbc | 1h 46m
A 19-year-old Anthony Bourdain travels to Provincetown and stumbles into the chaotic world of a restaurant kitchen, setting off a summer that will shape the course of his life.
Produced by A24.
name: Tony

description: 2025 - PG - 1hr 42 mins - In their latest adventure, the toys find their future under threat when their young owner, Bonnie, falls under the spell of a smart tablet.
name: Toy Story 5

description:
2026| 12A | 1h 35m
London 1910: a passionate astronomer does everything she can to avoid romantic love and marriage. Her bold challenge to Edwardian patriarchy is set against the suffragette movement and advances in science and technology.
name: Virginia Woolf's Night and Day
